meaning: n.有抱负者,有野心者adj.上进的,有野心的
examples: People like Vicky and her friends represent the leading edge, the trailblazers for a huge mass of young, eagerly aspirant consumers.
If you say so, I'll regard him not an aspirant man.
They appeal to, and are priced for, India’s aspirant middle class.
The leading GOP presidential aspirant promoted monetary reform as core to the economic reform agenda last week.
Mr. Romney is hardly the first Republican presidential aspirant to take that tack against a Democratic incumbent.
